REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION, 501, DEFENDANT BRIAN ZHU'S MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO PRESENT ANY FACTS DEMONSTRATING THAT THE JUDGMENT TAKEN AGAINST HIM WAS THROUGH HIS MISTAKE OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. (C.C.P. 473(B).) DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENT THAT HE WAS CONFUSED BY THE "ET AL" DESIGNATION IS UNDERMINED BY THE FACT THAT HE FILED AN ANSWER TO THE COMPLAINT AND DOES NOT DISPUTE THAT HE RECEIVED NOTICE OF TIME AND PLACE OF TRIAL THAT CLEARLY STATED THAT TRIAL WAS SET FOR JANUARY 26, 2026 AND THAT "ALL PARTIES MUST APPEAR ON THE DATE OF TRIAL. DEFENDANT FAILS TO CLEARLY EXPLAIN WHY HE DID NOT INQUIRE INTO THE DATE ON THE NOTICE OR WHY HE FAILED TO APPEAR. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, NOT REPORTED (501/CFH)
PROOF OF SERVICE OF REPLY TO OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT; AND DECLARATION FILED BY DEFENDANT APPLEBAUM, NAOMI

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT; IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS THE SERVICE BY PUBLICATION ORDER WAS NOT ISSUED IN ERROR AND THAT SERVICE WAS COMPLETE ON THE LAST DAY OF REQUIRED PUBLICATION. CONTRARY TO DEFENDANT'S CLAIMS, PERSONAL SERVICE WAS ATTEMPTED AT DEFENDANT'S ADDRESS BY THE ALAMEDA SHERIFF ON 5 SEPARATE OCCASIONS. SUBSTITUTE SERVICE BY MAILING WAS COMPLETED IN FEBRUARY 2024. DEFENDANT CONCEDES THAT SHE RECEIVED THE NOTICE AND ACKNOWLEDGEMENT FORM BUT CHOSE NOT TO COMPLETE IT AND RETURN THE FORM EVEN AFTER SPEAKING WITH PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL. DEFENDANT ALSO CONCEDES THE REQUEST FOR DEFAULT WAS MAILED ON AUGUST 8, 2024 TO HER CORRECT ADDRESS. THE COURT FINDS SERVICE OF THE SUMMONS RESULTED IN ACTUAL NOTICE TO DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT NAOMI APPLEBAUM Motion To Set Aside Default;

is DENIED. The Court finds the Service by Publication Order was not issued in error and that service was complete on the last day of required publication. Contrary to Defendant's claims, personal service was attempted at Defendant's address by the Alameda Sheriff on 5 separate occasions. Substitute service by mailing was completed in February 2024. Defendant concedes that she received the Notice and Acknowledgement form but chose not to complete it and return the form even after speaking with Plaintiff's counsel. Defendant also concedes the Request for Default was mailed on August 8, 2024 to her correct address. The Court finds service of the summons resulted in actual notice to Defendant.

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BRIAN ZHU'S MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT

Appearances:

Wooksun Hong of the Law Offices of Wooksun Hong (510.368.8538) for plaintiff and opposing party;

Brian Zhu (510.206.3860) defendant and moving party appearing in propria persona.

Argued and the court makes the following ruling:

DENIED. Defendant failed to present any facts demonstrating that the Judgment taken against him was through his mistake or excusable neglect. (C.C.P. 473(b).) Defendant's argument that he was confused by the "et al" designation is undermined by the fact that he filed an Answer to the Complaint and does not dispute that he received Notice of Time and Place of Trial that clearly stated that trial was set for January 26, 2026 and that "all parties MUST appear on the date of trial. Defendant fails to clearly explain why he did not inquire into the date on the Notice or why he failed to appear. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: w. Trupek, Not Reported (501/CFH)
